known_gaps: The protected Class AB headphone path now passes schematic electrical validation, PCB realization, placement, and endpoint binding with verified LMV321 op-amp, diode, and output-transistor component selections.
The board envelope is intentionally larger for this fixture so realized amplifier block placements remain inside the outline without weakening placement validation for undersized boards.
Routing is now enabled for this fixture and produces explicit route-tree evidence instead of a stale skip policy.
The current hard blocker has advanced to routed-board validation: routing and project write complete, but internal amplifier/output nets still report disconnected pads and partial routes.
Routing summaries retain route-tree and required-net evidence so AI repair loops can target the remaining disconnected output drive, emitter, load reference, and supply nets.

Output protection remains connectivity-level: fault_protection_status is placeholder_blocked, active fault protection is not implemented, HP_RET-to-LOAD_REF load-return policy is not promoted, and separated LOAD_REF-to-GND net-tie evidence is not proven.

Amplifier output transistor evidence remains warning-only for fabrication promotion: power dissipation, thermal review, safe operating area, and bias-diode thermal placement evidence are still review_required.
Real KiCad ERC/DRC evidence remains skipped until routed-board validation is clean enough for this fixture to become a KiCad-backed candidate.
notes: Optional protected Class AB headphone amplifier fixture that captures the first KiCad-backed evidence gap after schematic alias cleanup, placement fit, endpoint binding, routing enablement, and project write: routed-board validation still reports disconnected pads and partial internal amplifier/output nets. Configuring KICADAI_KICAD_CLI should not promote this fixture until validation is clean enough to reach kicad_checks. DRC is intentionally not required while the fixture remains validation-blocked.
